zh.Chipping Barnet, Hertfordshire, England: 英國賀特福爾其皮三 巴內特
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe於1506年9月11日出生。她於1505年與Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House在英國英格蘭貝德福爾亨洛福結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1563年8月28日享年56歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe于1506年9月11日出生。她于1505年与Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House在英国英格兰貝德福爾亨洛福结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1563年8月28日享年56岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可。

nameMeanings.name: Walker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly North and Midlands) and Scottish: occupational name for a fuller, from Middle English walker, Old English wealcere (an agent derivative of wealcan ‘to walk, tread’), ‘one who trampled cloth in a bath of lye or kneaded it, in order to strengthen it’. This was the regular term for the occupation during the Middle Ages in western and northern England. Compare Fuller and Tucker . As a Scottish surname it has also been used as a translation of Gaelic Mac an Fhucadair ‘son of the fuller’. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

History: The name was brought to North America from northern England and Scotland independently by many different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Samuel Walker came to Lynn, MA, c. 1630; Philip Walker was in Rehoboth, MA, in or before 1643. The surname was also established in VA before 1650; a Thomas Walker, born in 1715 in King and Queen County, VA, was a physician, soldier, and explorer.

variants.name: Walcher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gunther, Hans, Horst, Klaus.
German: variant of   Walker   or   Walch  .

variants.name: Waker
meanings: English (Yorkshire):
either a nickname for a watchful person or an occupational name for a watchman, from Middle English waker ‘watchful, vigilant’ (Old English wacor) and the derived noun.
mostly an early Modern English and Scots variant of   Walker  .

variants.name: Fuller
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Walke
meanings: German: metonymic occupational name for a fuller, from Middle High German walke ‘fulling mill’. Compare Walker .
English (Devon): variant of   Walk  .

variants.name: Walck
meanings: German: variant of   Walker   or   Walch  .

nameMeanings.name: Marshe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from either of two places so called in North Yorkshire, name with Old English mersc ‘marsh’, the -sk being the result of Scandinavian influence.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘Morning dew’; also ‘generosity’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe was born on 11 September 1506. She married Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House in 1505, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 28 August 1563, at the age of 56, and was buried in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe est née le 11 septembre 1506. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House en 1505, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 28 août 1563, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe wurde am 11. September 1506 geboren. Sie hat 1505 in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 28. August 1563 im Alter von 56 Jahren und wurde in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe è nata il il 11 settembre 1506. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House nel 1505, nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 28 agosto 1563, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolta a Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marsheは1506年9月11日に生まれた。1505年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウでSir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy Houseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1563年8月28日に56歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe(은)는 1506년 9월 11일 태어났다. 그녀는 1505년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1563년 8월 28일 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe родилась 11 сентября 1506 г. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House в 1505 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 28 августа 1563 г. в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.
